source: CEFR A2 descriptors + standard reference French grammar (Grammaire progressive du français, CLE International)

cards.title: Habits & routines
cards.goal: Can describe habits, routines and daily life in connected sentences.
functions: décrire des habitudes et des routines
grammar: présent, toutes personnes (incl. verbes irréguliers fréquents: aller, faire, prendre)
adverbes et expressions de fréquence, place dans la phrase
cards.evidence: Speak for 60 seconds about a typical week using at least four different frequency expressions, correctly placed.

cards.title: Describing people
cards.goal: Can describe a person's appearance and personality in simple terms.
grammar: avoir for physical features: il a les yeux bleus, elle a les cheveux longs
cards.evidence: Describe two people the listener doesn't know (one appearance-focused with avoir, one personality-focused with être) so the listener can summarise them back.
cards.note: Physical features use avoir + definite article (il a LES yeux bleus), not être — a direct transfer trap from languages that use 'to be' throughout.

cards.title: Describing things & objects
cards.goal: Can describe everyday objects: appearance, material, purpose.
grammar: c'est fait de… / ça sert à + infinitif
adjectifs de matière et de forme
cards.evidence: Mystery object: describe three objects without naming them (material, size, purpose each); listener guesses.

cards.title: Describing a place
cards.goal: Can give an extended simple description of a place: where it is, what it has, what it's known for.
cards.evidence: Describe a place the listener has never visited in 5–6 connected sentences: location, size, two features, one thing it's known for.

cards.title: Likes, dislikes & preferences
cards.goal: Can explain what he/she likes or dislikes about something and express wants politely.
functions: exprimer des goûts et des préférences
grammar: ça me plaît / ça ne me plaît pas (plaire construction)
cards.evidence: Compare two options giving two likes and two dislikes with reasons, using at least one plaire-construction sentence, then state a preference.
cards.note: Plaire works like Spanish gustar or English 'appeal to' — the thing liked is the grammatical subject (ce film me plaît = this film pleases me), not the person. A structurally alien pattern worth isolating.

cards.title: Telling a simple past story
cards.goal: Can describe past activities and personal experiences as a short connected narrative.
cards.evidence: Tell a 90-second true story using at least three sequencing linkers, then answer two follow-up questions.

cards.title: Setting the scene
cards.goal: Can distinguish background situation from main events when narrating.
grammar: imparfait vs passé composé, first contact: imparfait for background, passé composé for events
cards.evidence: Retell an incident with at least two scene-setting imparfait clauses interrupted by a passé composé event.
skin_examples: what was happening when something surprising occurred

cards.title: How things used to be
cards.goal: Can contrast past habits and states with the present.
grammar: imparfait for repeated past habits and states
cards.evidence: Then vs now: four sentences contrasting a past habit with present reality, imparfait vs présent correctly split.

cards.title: Life experiences (first contact)
cards.goal: Can ask and answer about life experiences without specifying when.
grammar: déjà / jamais / pas encore + passé composé
cards.evidence: Have-you-ever exchange: ask and answer five experience questions with déjà/jamais/pas encore, switching correctly to passé composé follow-ups with a specific time.
cards.note: Full nuance (depuis, venir de, present-vs-passé-composé for ongoing situations) is FR-B1-04/05.

cards.title: Plans & intentions
cards.goal: Can describe plans and arrangements.
functions: parler de projets et de rendez-vous
grammar: présent for fixed arrangements (on part demain)
cards.evidence: Describe next week: three intentions (aller + infinitif) and two fixed arrangements (present tense), correctly distinguished.

cards.title: Predictions & spontaneous decisions
cards.goal: Can make simple predictions and express decisions made at the moment of speaking.
grammar: futur simple vs aller + infinitif (prediction/opinion vs near-certain/evidence-based)
cards.evidence: React to five prompt situations choosing futur simple or aller+infinitif appropriately; justify one choice.

cards.title: Suggestions & invitations
cards.goal: Can make and respond to suggestions and invitations; can discuss what to do and where to go and arrange to meet.
cards.evidence: Consensus roleplay: plan an outing from three options; each side makes two suggestions and rejects one politely.

cards.title: Requests & offers
cards.goal: Can make polite requests and offers and respond to them.
cards.evidence: Three-scene roleplay: borrowing something, asking for help, offering help, with appropriate politeness in each.

cards.title: Advice
cards.goal: Can give and ask for simple advice.
cards.evidence: Respond to three friend-problems with one devrais, one ne-devrais-pas and one pourrais-essayer suggestion, each with a reason.

cards.title: Obligation & necessity
cards.goal: Can express what is necessary, obligatory or forbidden.
cards.evidence: Explain the rules of something familiar: two il faut, two devoir, two il est interdit de.
cards.note: Keep this to il faut/devoir + infinitive at A2; il faut QUE + subjonctif is the FR-B1-19 upgrade.

cards.title: Possibility
cards.goal: Can express uncertainty about present and future situations.
cards.evidence: Speculate about three open questions using at least three different possibility expressions.
skin_examples: what a friend is doing right now
how an event will turn out
cards.note: Subjunctive-triggering possibility phrases (il se peut que, il est possible que) are deferred to FR-B1-14 — keep this card indicative-only.

cards.title: Shops, travel & services
cards.goal: Can make simple transactions in shops and travel situations: goods, prices, tickets, directions, opening times.
grammar: quantifieurs: beaucoup de, un peu de, quelques, plusieurs, assez de
cards.evidence: Two back-to-back transactional roleplays (a shop and a travel situation) completed without L1 and without breakdown.

cards.title: Purpose & reasons
cards.goal: Can explain why he/she does something or wants something.
grammar: verbe + infinitif: vouloir, espérer, décider de
cards.evidence: Give five why-answers about own life, each using a purpose infinitive or parce-que clause, with five different reasons.

cards.title: Real conditions & consequences
cards.goal: Can express general truths and likely future consequences.
cards.evidence: Produce three si+présent+présent sentences (facts/rules) and three si+présent+futur sentences (real plans/warnings), correctly distinguished.

cards.title: Comparing
cards.goal: Can compare people, places and things.
cards.evidence: Rank three items best to worst with two comparative and two superlative sentences justifying the ranking, agreement correct.
